title: P848 Endpoint Availability Staircase Theorem v0

theoremObject.statement: For any exact Problem 848 endpoint repair handoff whose endpoint selector is affine k_p(t) = a*t + b mod p^2 over a finite residue parameter t mod p^2 and a fixed window W, the available residues A = {t : k_p(t) <= floor((W - 14) / 25)} and unavailable residues U = {t : k_p(t) > floor((W - 14) / 25)} form an exact finite partition of the source class. Available children may advance only through a deterministic square-obstruction child, an exact-certified repair handoff, or an explicit blocker/open leaf. The unavailable complement must remain an explicit open obligation until a separate cover or impossibility theorem accounts for it.
theoremObject.notGlobalClosure: This theorem compresses the repeated endpoint availability split and handoff mechanism. It does not cover any unavailable complement, does not prove a globally decreasing endpoint tree, and does not decide Problem 848.

partitionArgument: For a fixed endpoint prime p, the parameter t is finite modulo p^2.
The selector k_p(t) is evaluated as its least residue in {0, ..., p^2 - 1}.
The inequalities k_p(t) <= floor((W - 14) / 25) and k_p(t) > floor((W - 14) / 25) are complementary and disjoint for every residue t.
Therefore A and U partition the source class exactly. When gcd(a, p^2) = 1, the selector is a permutation, so |A| = floor((W - 14) / 25) + 1 and |U| = p^2 - |A|.
handoffArgument: An available residue family is not a closure by itself.
A deterministic square-obstruction child is allowed only when a packet records an exact CRT/lifted class and q^2 divisibility witness.
A repair handoff is allowed only when a packet records an exact squarefree factor certificate.
A bounded trial survivor without exact certification remains a repair candidate or blocker/open leaf.
An unavailable complement never disappears from the proof surface; it must be covered, proved impossible, or ledgered as open.
proof.relationshipToTransitionRule: This theorem instantiates p848_frontier_transition_rule_v0 for endpoint availability partitions: partition transitions are finite and typed, available-child transitions are gated by exact witnesses/certificates, and unavailable complements remain visible obligations.

proofBoundary: The endpoint availability staircase theorem v0 proves the exact finite split/handoff discipline. It does not cover the p4217 unavailable complement, does not prove the staircase terminates globally, does not certify p97 or p227, and does not bind the synthetic 282/841 row to a live family-menu row.
